goal: Stop the Celtic poster narration from overflowing/shrinking in Thai, and drop dead code
done: Added a 130-char (space-breaking) cap to firstSentenceForNarration so Thai (no sentence-final punctuation) doesn't return whole bodies; removed the now-dead drawInsightPanel. share/poster.js.
symptom: The new Celtic narration was built from each position's first sentence via a .!? split. Thai interpretations have no terminal punctuation, so Thai got each position's entire body (~180 chars ×4 ≈ 720) — the shrink-to-fit font hit its floor or the reading truncated.
root_cause: firstSentenceForNarration relied solely on Latin sentence-final punctuation to bound each position's contribution.
fix: Cap each contribution at 130 chars, breaking at the last space (ellipsis if cut) — language-agnostic; also guards runaway EN sentences.
verified: python replication on real data/cards.json celtic_cross_*_{en,th}: EN narration ~404 chars (lead sentences, unchanged), TH ~422 chars (was ~720), each capped at a clean phrase boundary
drawInsightPanel refs = 0 after removal; wrapText still used (9x)
affects: Celtic Cross poster narration, Thai especially
filed: chrome-devtools MCP browser disconnected mid-session — visual poster QA (incl. real card art + on-device) still pending for Dunkin
notes: Verified by string-logic on real data (browser was unavailable for a fresh render this pass). Mirror sync deferred (BUG-015).
